## 0x101: MCC\_CONFIG.GDRV

Default: 0x81003431

Gate drive configurations.

| BITS & NAME             | TYPE & RESET   | DESCRIPTION                        |
|-------------------------|----------------|------------------------------------|
| [31] CHARGE_PUMP_EN     | RW 0x1         | Enable or disable the charge pump. |
| [28] LP_MODE_EN         | RW 0x0         | Low power mode.                    |
| [24] USE_INTERNAL_R_REF | RW 0x1         | Use internal reference resistor.   |

| BITS & NAME            | TYPE & RESET   | TYPE & RESET                                                                                                                           | DESCRIPTION                                                                                                                                                                                       |
|------------------------|----------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| [20] OVERTEMP_LATCH    | RW 0x0         | RW 0x0                                                                                                                                 | Overtemperature fail events are latched in the CHIP.EVENTS.OT_FAIL_EVENT register bit. This bit controls whether or not the event bit has to be cleared before the driver stage is enabled again. |
| [20] OVERTEMP_LATCH    | 0: 1:          | AUTO_RESTART Automatic restart after overtemperature status bit is cleared. CLEAR Overtemperature event bit must be cleared before     | AUTO_RESTART Automatic restart after overtemperature status bit is cleared. CLEAR Overtemperature event bit must be cleared before                                                                |
| [16] DRV_EN_BIT        | RW 0x0         |                                                                                                                                        | Enable or disable the driver stage. Any motor motion operation requires the driver stage to be enabled (DRV_EN_BIT=1 and DRV_EN input high).                                                      |
| [13:12] OCP_AUTORETRY  | RW 0x3         |                                                                                                                                        | Overcurrent protection (OCP) retry time. When set, the gate driver is automatically restarted some time after an OCP event occurs indefinitely.                                                   |
| [13:12] OCP_AUTORETRY  | 0: 1: 2: 3:    | RETRY_AFTER_1MS Retry after 1 ms. RETRY_AFTER_5MS Retry after 5 ms. RETRY_AFTER_50MS Retry after 50 ms. NO_RETRY No retry is executed. | RETRY_AFTER_1MS Retry after 1 ms. RETRY_AFTER_5MS Retry after 5 ms. RETRY_AFTER_50MS Retry after 50 ms. NO_RETRY No retry is executed.                                                            |
| [11:10] OCP_DEGLITCH   | RW 0x1         |                                                                                                                                        | Overcurrent protection (OCP) deglitch time. OCP events are triggered only if the overcurrent state is present this time after the overcurrent status is detected.                                 |
| [11:10] OCP_DEGLITCH   | 0: 1: 2: 3:    | DEGLITCH_300_NS 300 ns DEGLITCH_600_NS 600 ns DEGLITCH_1200_NS 1200 ns DEGLITCH_2400_NS 2400 ns                                        | DEGLITCH_300_NS 300 ns DEGLITCH_600_NS 600 ns DEGLITCH_1200_NS 1200 ns DEGLITCH_2400_NS 2400 ns                                                                                                   |
| [9] OCP_DETECTION_MODE | RW 0x0         |                                                                                                                                        | Overcurrent protection (OCP) detected by monitoring load current or output voltage                                                                                                                |
| [9] OCP_DETECTION_MODE | 0: 1:          | LOAD_CURRENT OCP checks load current. OUTPUT_VOLTAGE OCP checks output voltage.                                                        | LOAD_CURRENT OCP checks load current. OUTPUT_VOLTAGE OCP checks output voltage.                                                                                                                   |
| [5:4] LS_RES_ON        | RW 0x3         |                                                                                                                                        | Low side power resistor Ron selection.                                                                                                                                                            |
| [5:4] LS_RES_ON        | 0: 1: 2: 3:    | RES_200_MOHM 200 mOhm RES_112_MOHM 112 mOhm RES_78_MOHM 78 mOhm RES_50_MOHM 55 mOhm                                                    | RES_200_MOHM 200 mOhm RES_112_MOHM 112 mOhm RES_78_MOHM 78 mOhm RES_50_MOHM 55 mOhm                                                                                                               |
| [1:0] SLEW_RATE        | RW 0x1         |                                                                                                                                        | Control output slew rate.                                                                                                                                                                         |
| [1:0] SLEW_RATE        | 0: 1: 2:       | SR_100_V_PER_US 100 V/µs. SR_200_V_PER_US 200 V/µs. SR_400_V_PER_US 400 V/µs. SR_800_V_PER_US 800 V/µs.                                | SR_100_V_PER_US 100 V/µs. SR_200_V_PER_US 200 V/µs. SR_400_V_PER_US 400 V/µs. SR_800_V_PER_US 800 V/µs.                                                                                           |

## 0x102: MCC\_CONFIG.PWM

Default: 0x00000017

General configurations for the PWM engine.

| BITS & NAME                | TYPE & RESET                   | DESCRIPTION                                                                                             |
|----------------------------|--------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| [31:16] FLAT_BOTTOM_OFFSET | RW, unsigned 0x0000            | Offset for PWM duty cycle for flat bottom modulation with offset mode, used when SV_MODE=BOTTOM_OFFSET. |
| [5:4] SV_MODE              | RW 0x1 0: DISABLED 1: HARMONIC | Space Vector PWM Mode. Space Vector PWM disabled. Third Harmonic Injection enabled.                     |

| BITS & NAME   | TYPE & RESET                                                                                                      | DESCRIPTION                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
|---------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| [2:0] CHOP    | 0x7 Other 0: OFF_FREE 1: OFF_LSON 2: OFF_HSON 3: OFF_FREE2 4: OFF_FREE3 5: LSPWM_HSOFF 6: HSPWM_LSOFF 7: CENTERED | modes are for testing purposes. PWM off, free running. PWM off, Low Side (LS) permanently on. PWM off, High Side (HS) permanent on. PWM off, free running. PWM off, free running. Low side (LS) PWM, high side (HS) off. High side (HS) PWM, low side (LS) off. Centered PWM for FOC. |

## 0x103: MCC\_CONFIG.PWM\_PERIOD

Default: 0xFFFF12C0

Configuration of the PWM frequency as well as the auto Kirchhoff duty cycle limit.

| BITS & NAME               | TYPE & RESET        | DESCRIPTION                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
|---------------------------|---------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| [31:16] AUTO_KIRCHOFF_LIM | RW, unsigned 0xFFFF | If the PWM duty cycle of any phase exceeds this limit, calculate the third current using Kirchhoff rule instead of the ADC measurement of that phase as it might be corrupted.                                                                                                              |
| [15:0] MAX_COUNT          | RW, unsigned 0x12C0 | Number of 120 MHz clock cycles from which the PWM signal that drives the output stage is generated. Determines the PWM frequency as well as the FOC controller main update rate. Calculate with MAX_COUNT = 120MHz / f_pwm. The default value of 4800 results in a PWM frequency of 25 kHz. |

## 0x104: MCC\_CONFIG.BRAKE\_CHOPPER\_LIMITS

Default: 0xFFFF0000

Threshold values with which the internal measured supply voltage is compared to control the brake chopper output to be active as soon as a soft overvoltage limit is exceeded.

| BITS & NAME              | TYPE & RESET        | DESCRIPTION                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
|--------------------------|---------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| [31:16] UPPER_SWITCH_LIM | RW, unsigned 0xFFFF | Upper switching limit for brake chopper of VM measurement. Exceeding this limit activates OV_VM_LIMIT_FAIL_STATUS status bit resp. BRAKE output if this output is enabled by CHIP.IO_MATRIX.                                                                                          |
| [15:0] LOWER_SWITCH_LIM  | RW, unsigned 0x0000 | Lower switching limit for brake chopper of VM measurement (low hysteresis value). During overvoltage condition (active OV_VM_LIMIT_FAIL_STATUS bit), falling below this limit deactivates OV_VM_LIMIT_FAIL_STATUS bit resp. BRAKE output if this output is enabled by CHIP.IO_MATRIX. |

## 0x105: MCC\_CONFIG.MCC\_STATUS

Default: 0x00000000

Status and event bits related to the driver stage.

| BITS & NAME             | TYPE & RESET   | DESCRIPTION                        |
|-------------------------|----------------|------------------------------------|
| [24] OVERTEMP_CH3_EVENT | RW, W1C 0x0    | Overtemperature channel 3 detected |
| [23] OVERTEMP_CH2_EVENT | RW, W1C 0x0    | Overtemperature channel 2 detected |
| [22] OVERTEMP_CH1_EVENT | RW, W1C 0x0    | Overtemperature channel 1 detected |
| [21] SHRT2V_CH3_EVENT   | RW, W1C 0x0    | Shrt-to-VS channel 3 detected      |

| BITS & NAME             | TYPE & RESET   | DESCRIPTION                       |
|-------------------------|----------------|-----------------------------------|
| [20] SHRT2V_CH2_EVENT   | RW, W1C 0x0    | Shrt-to-VS channel 2 detected     |
| [19] SHRT2V_CH1_EVENT   | RW, W1C 0x0    | Shrt-to-VS channel 1 detected     |
| [18] SHRT2G_CH3_EVENT   | RW, W1C 0x0    | Shrt-to-Ground channel 3 detected |
| [17] SHRT2G_CH2_EVENT   | RW, W1C 0x0    | Shrt-to-Ground channel 2 detected |
| [16] SHRT2G_CH1_EVENT   | RW, W1C 0x0    | Shrt-to-Ground channel 1 detected |
| [8] OVERTEMP_CH3_STATUS | R 0x0          | Overtemperature channel 3         |
| [7] OVERTEMP_CH2_STATUS | R 0x0          | Overtemperature channel 2         |
| [6] OVERTEMP_CH1_STATUS | R 0x0          | Overtemperature channel 1         |
| [5] SHRT2V_CH3_STATUS   | R 0x0          | Shrt-to-VS channel 3              |
| [4] SHRT2V_CH2_STATUS   | R 0x0          | Shrt-to-VS channel 2              |
| [3] SHRT2V_CH1_STATUS   | R 0x0          | Shrt-to-VS channel 1              |
| [2] SHRT2G_CH3_STATUS   | R 0x0          | Shrt-to-Ground channel 3          |
| [1] SHRT2G_CH2_STATUS   | R 0x0          | Shrt-to-Ground channel 2          |
| [0] SHRT2G_CH1_STATUS   | R 0x0          | Shrt-to-Ground channel 1          |

## 0x106: MCC\_CONFIG.TORQUE\_FF\_ACC\_CONFIG

Default: 0x00060000

Configuration of the torque feedforward acceleration component.

| BITS & NAME            | TYPE & RESET                                                                                                 | DESCRIPTION                                                                                                                                                                           |
|------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| [18:16] RAMP_ACC_SHIFT | RW 0x6                                                                                                       | Shift factor to the ramper acceleration for torque feedforward acceleration component calculation. torque_ff_acc = (RAMPER.ACCELERATION × RAMP_ACC_GAIN) >> (RAMP_ACC_SHIFT × 4)      |
| [18:16] RAMP_ACC_SHIFT | 0: NO_ACC_SHIFT 1: ACC_SHIFT_BY_4 2: ACC_SHIFT_BY_8 3: ACC_SHIFT_BY_12 4: ACC_SHIFT_BY_16 5: ACC_SHIFT_BY_20 | 0: NO_ACC_SHIFT 1: ACC_SHIFT_BY_4 2: ACC_SHIFT_BY_8 3: ACC_SHIFT_BY_12 4: ACC_SHIFT_BY_16 5: ACC_SHIFT_BY_20                                                                          |
| [15:0] RAMP_ACC_GAIN   | RW, signed 0x0000                                                                                            | Gain factor to scale the ramper acceleration for torque feedforward acceleration component calculation. torque_ff_acc = (RAMPER.ACCELERATION × RAMP_ACC_GAIN) >> (RAMP_ACC_SHIFT × 4) |

## 0x107: MCC\_CONFIG.TORQUE\_FF\_VISC\_FRIC\_CONFIG

Default: 0x00070000

Configuration of the torque feedforward viscous friction component.

| BITS & NAME            | TYPE & RESET                                                                                                                                                         | DESCRIPTION                                                                                                                                                                             |
|------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| [18:16] RAMP_VEL_SHIFT | RW 0x7                                                                                                                                                               | Shift factor to the ramper velocity for torque feedforward viscous friction component calculation. torque_ff_visc_fric = (RAMPER.V_ACTUAL × RAMP_VEL_GAIN) >> (RAMP_VEL_SHIFT × 4)      |
| RAMP_VEL_GAIN          | 0: NO_VEL_SHIFT 1: VEL_SHIFT_BY_4 2: VEL_SHIFT_BY_8 3: VEL_SHIFT_BY_12 4: VEL_SHIFT_BY_16 5: VEL_SHIFT_BY_20 6: VEL_SHIFT_BY_24 7: VEL_SHIFT_BY_28 RW, signed 0x0000 | Gain factor to scale the ramper velocity for torque feedforward viscous friction component calculation. torque_ff_visc_fric = (RAMPER.V_ACTUAL × RAMP_VEL_GAIN) >> (RAMP_VEL_SHIFT × 4) |

## 0x108: MCC\_CONFIG.TORQUE\_FF\_COLOUMB\_FRIC

Default: 0x00000000

Configuration of the torque feedforward Coulomb friction component.

| BITS & NAME                   | TYPE & RESET      | DESCRIPTION                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |
|-------------------------------|-------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| [15:0] TORQUE_FF_COLOUMB_FRIC | RW, signed 0x0000 | Feedforward value for the torque feedforward Coulomb friction component. The value in this field is used for torque_ff_coulomb_fric if RAMPER.V_ACTUAL > 0; the inverted value is used if RAMPER.V_ACTUAL < 0; and torque_ff_coulomb_fric = 0 otherwise. |

## 0x109: MCC\_CONFIG.TORQUE\_FEEDFORWARD

Default: 0x00000000

Feedforward value for torque PID controller.

| BITS & NAME               | TYPE & RESET     | DESCRIPTION                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |
|---------------------------|------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| [15:0] TORQUE_FEEDFORWARD | R, signed 0x0000 | Feedforward value for torque PID controller. Depending on the enabled torque feedforward components in MCC_CONFIG.MOTOR_MOTION register, this value results from: torque_ff_visc_fric + torque_ff_coulomb_fric + torque_ff_acc. |

0x140: FOC.PID\_CONFIG

Default: 0x0000855C

General configuration for the PID controller.

| BITS & NAME               | TYPE & RESET      | DESCRIPTION                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |
|---------------------------|-------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| [22:16] POSITION_SAMPLING | RW, unsigned 0x00 | Set the sampling frequency for the position controller, calculated as position_controller_sampling_freq = pwm_freq / (VELOCITY_SAMPLING + 1) / (POSITION_SAMPLING + 1). VELOCITY_SAMPLING is part of FEEDBACK.VELOCITY_FRQ_CONF. |
| [15:12] VELOCITY_SHIFT    | RW, unsigned 0x8  | Shift factor for the velocity controller output. The shifted value becomes the target of the torque controller when MOTION_MODE = VELOCITY or MOTION_MODE = POSITION. Shift right by VELOCITY_SHIFT bits.                        |

| BITS & NAME             | TYPE & RESET                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 | DESCRIPTION                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |
|-------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| [11:10] POSITION_NORM_I | RW 0x1                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       | Normalization factor for the Ki coefficient of the position controller. Change the fixed-point interpretation of the POSITION_I field from default Q8.8 (POSITION_NORM_I = 0) by 8 bits right for each POSITON_NORM_I number increase. This effectively divides the coefficient I value in the register field by 256^(POSITION_NORM_I+1), keeping the fractional part of the result.         |
| [11:10] POSITION_NORM_I | 0: I_SHIFT_BY_8 1: I_SHIFT_BY_16 2: I_SHIFT_BY_24 3: I_SHIFT_BY_32                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           | Position Shift Factor Ki: Shifts POSITION_I right by 8 bits Position Shift Factor Ki: Shifts POSITION_I right by 16 bits Position Shift Factor Ki: Shifts POSITION_I right by 24 bits Position Shift Factor Ki: Shifts POSITION_I right by 32 bits                                                                                                                                           |
| [9:8] POSITION_NORM_P   | RW 0x1 Normalization factor for the Kp coefficient of the position controller. Change the fixed-point interpretation of the POSITION_P field from default Q16.0 (POSITION_NORM_P = 0) by 8 bits right for each POSITON_NORM_P number increase. This effectively divides the coefficient P value in the register field by 256^POSITION_NORM_P, keeping the fractional part of the result.     | RW 0x1 Normalization factor for the Kp coefficient of the position controller. Change the fixed-point interpretation of the POSITION_P field from default Q16.0 (POSITION_NORM_P = 0) by 8 bits right for each POSITON_NORM_P number increase. This effectively divides the coefficient P value in the register field by 256^POSITION_NORM_P, keeping the fractional part of the result.     |
| [9:8] POSITION_NORM_P   | 0: P_NO_SHIFT 1: P_SHIFT_BY_8 2: P_SHIFT_BY_16 3: P_SHIFT_BY_24                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              | Position Shift Factor Kp: No shift for POSITION_P Position Shift Factor Kp: Shifts POSITION_P right by 8 bits Position Shift Factor Kp: Shifts POSITION_P right by 16 bits Position Shift Factor Kp: Shifts POSITION_P right by 24 bits                                                                                                                                                      |
| [7:6] VELOCITY_NORM_I   | Normalization factor for the Ki coefficient of the velocity controller. Change the fixed-point interpretation of the VELOCITY_I field from default Q8.8 (VELOCITY_NORM_I = 0) by 8 bits right for each VELOCITY_NORM_I number increase. This effectively divides the coefficient I value in the register field by 256^(VELOCITY_NORM_I+1), keeping the fractional part of                    | Normalization factor for the Ki coefficient of the velocity controller. Change the fixed-point interpretation of the VELOCITY_I field from default Q8.8 (VELOCITY_NORM_I = 0) by 8 bits right for each VELOCITY_NORM_I number increase. This effectively divides the coefficient I value in the register field by 256^(VELOCITY_NORM_I+1), keeping the fractional part of                    |
| [7:6] VELOCITY_NORM_I   | the 0: I_SHIFT_BY_8 1: I_SHIFT_BY_16 2: I_SHIFT_BY_24 3: I_SHIFT_BY_32                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       | result. Velocity Shift Factor Ki: Shifts VELOCITY_I right by 8 bits Velocity Shift Factor Ki: Shifts VELOCITY_I right by 16 bits Velocity Shift Factor Ki: Shifts VELOCITY_I right by 24 bits Velocity Shift Factor Ki: Shifts VELOCITY_I right by 32 bits                                                                                                                                   |
| [5:4] VELOCITY_NORM_P   | Normalization factor for the Kp coefficient of the velocity controller. Change the fixed-point interpretation of the VELOCITY_P field from default Q16.0 (VELOCITY_NORM_P = 0) by 8 bits right for each VELOCITY_NORM_P number increase. This effectively divides the coefficient I value in the register field by 256^VELOCITY_NORM_P, keeping the fractional part of the                   | Normalization factor for the Kp coefficient of the velocity controller. Change the fixed-point interpretation of the VELOCITY_P field from default Q16.0 (VELOCITY_NORM_P = 0) by 8 bits right for each VELOCITY_NORM_P number increase. This effectively divides the coefficient I value in the register field by 256^VELOCITY_NORM_P, keeping the fractional part of the                   |
| [5:4] VELOCITY_NORM_P   | result. 0: P_NO_SHIFT 1: P_SHIFT_BY_8 2: P_SHIFT_BY_16 3: P_SHIFT_BY_24 RW                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   | Velocity Shift Factor Kp: No shift for VELOCITY_P Velocity Shift Factor Kp: Shifts VELOCITY_P right by 8 bits Velocity Shift Factor Kp: Shifts VELOCITY_P right by 16 bits Velocity Shift Factor Kp: Shifts VELOCITY_P right by 24 bits                                                                                                                                                      |
| [3] CURRENT_NORM_I      | Normalization factor for the torque and flux controllers' Ki coefficients. Change the fixed-point interpretation of TORQUE_I and FLUX_I fields from Q16.0 (CURRENT_NORM_I = 0) by shifting the number of fractional bits by 8 bits right (=> Q8.8). This effectively divides the coefficient values in the register fields by 256^CURRENT_NORM_I, keeping the fractional part of the result. | Normalization factor for the torque and flux controllers' Ki coefficients. Change the fixed-point interpretation of TORQUE_I and FLUX_I fields from Q16.0 (CURRENT_NORM_I = 0) by shifting the number of fractional bits by 8 bits right (=> Q8.8). This effectively divides the coefficient values in the register fields by 256^CURRENT_NORM_I, keeping the fractional part of the result. |
| [3] CURRENT_NORM_I      | 0: CUR_I_NO_SHIFT 1: CUR_I_SHIFT_BY_8                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        | Current Shift Factor Ki: No shift Current Shift Factor Ki: Shifts FLUX_I and TORQUE_I right by 8 bits                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |

| BITS & NAME          | TYPE & RESET                          | DESCRIPTION                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |
|----------------------|---------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| [2] CURRENT_NORM_P   | RW 0x1                                | Normalization factor for the torque and flux controllers' Kp coefficients. Change the fixed-point interpretation of TORQUE_P and FLUX_P fields from Q16.0 (CURRENT_NORM_P = 0) by shifting the number of fractional bits by 8 bits right (=> Q8.8). This effectively divides the coefficient values in the register fields by 256^CURRENT_NORM_P, keeping the fractional part of the result. |
| [2] CURRENT_NORM_P   | 0: CUR_P_NO_SHIFT 1: CUR_P_SHIFT_BY_8 | Current Shift Factor Kp: No shift Current Shift Factor Kp: Shifts FLUX_P and TORQUE_P right by 8 bits                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |
| [1] OVERWRITE_TARGET | RW 0x0                                | When enabled, the controller targets (pid_velocity_target, pid_torque_target) and integrator (pid_velocity_integrator) are overwritten directly by the module outputs for the smooth motion mode transition.                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| [0] KEEP_POS_TARGET  | RW 0x0                                | When set, PID_POSITION_TARGET is not automatically overwritten when manually writing PID_POSITION_ACTUAL register.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |

|---------------|----------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------|
| [15:0]        | RW, unsigned   | Maximum permissible back-EMF motor voltage, beyond which the field- |
| U_S_MAX       | 0x7FFF         | weakening controller is activated.                                  |

|---------------------|-------------------|----------------------------------------------------------|

## 0x147: FOC.PID\_POSITION\_TOLERANCE

Default: 0x00000000

Configuration of the position tolerance of the controller.

| BITS & NAME                   | TYPE & RESET            | DESCRIPTION                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |
|-------------------------------|-------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| [30:0] PID_POSITION_TOLERANCE | RW, unsigned 0x00000000 | The position controller ignores position errors smaller than this value. Two conditions must be true for this to take effect: the position_reached_event interrupt signal must be set and the error must remain smaller for at least the configured PID_POSITION_TOLERANCE_DELAY PWM cycles. |

## 0x148: FOC.PID\_POSITION\_TOLERANCE\_DELAY

Default: 0x00000000

Configuration of the timings for the position tolerance of the controller.

| BITS & NAME                         | TYPE & RESET        | DESCRIPTION                                                                                                                                                            |
|-------------------------------------|---------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| [15:0] PID_POSITION_TOLERANCE_DELAY | RW, unsigned 0x0000 | Number of PWM periods the absolute PID_POSITION_ERROR value must stay within configured PID_POSITION_TOLERANCE value for the position controller to ignore the errors. |

Default: 0x00005A81

Configuration of limits for the target values of the voltage controller.

| BITS & NAME             | TYPE & RESET        | DESCRIPTION                                                           |
|-------------------------|---------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------|
| [15:0] PID_UQ_UD_LIMITS | RW, unsigned 0x5A81 | Limit value for the absolute target values of the voltage controller. |

| [14:0] PID_FLUX_LIMIT    | RW, unsigned 0x7FFF | Limit for the absolute target value of the flux controller.   |

## 0x14C: FOC.PID\_POSITION\_LIMIT\_LOW

Default: 0x80000001

Configuration of lower limit for the target value of the position controller.

| BITS & NAME            | TYPE & RESET   | DESCRIPTION                                                                   |
|------------------------|----------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| [31:0]                 | RW, signed     | Lower limit for the target value of the position controller. The limit can be |
| PID_POSITION_LIMIT_LOW | 0x80000001     | positive or negative, but must be lower than PID_POSITION_LIMIT_HIGH.         |

## 0x14D: FOC.PID\_POSITION\_LIMIT\_HIGH

Default: 0x7FFFFFFF

Configuration of upper limit for the target value of the position controller.

| BITS & NAME             | TYPE & RESET   | DESCRIPTION                                                                   |
|-------------------------|----------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| [31:0]                  | RW, signed     | Upper limit for the target value of the position controller. The limit can be |
| PID_POSITION_LIMIT_HIGH | 0x7FFFFFFF     | positive or negative, but must be higher than PID_POSITION_LIMIT_LOW.         |

Default: 0x00000000

Current state of the FOC module.

## 0x171: FOC.U\_S\_ACTUAL\_I\_S\_ACTUAL

Default: 0x00000000

Actual motor voltage and current values.

| BITS & NAME   | TYPE & RESET   | DESCRIPTION                               |
|---------------|----------------|-------------------------------------------|
| [31:16]       | R, unsigned    | Actual motor voltage value, calculated as |
| U_S_ACTUAL    | 0x0000         | U_S_ACTUAL = sqrt(u_d^2 + u_q^2).         |
| [15:0]        | R, unsigned    | Actual motor current value, calculated as |
| I_S_ACTUAL    | 0x0000         | I_S_ACTUAL = sqrt(i_d^2 + i_q^2).         |

## 0x172: FOC.P\_MOTOR

Default: 0x00000000

Actual power applied to the motor.

| BITS & NAME   | TYPE & RESET   | DESCRIPTION                                      |
|---------------|----------------|--------------------------------------------------|
| [31:0]        | R, unsigned    | Actual power applied to the motor, calculated as |
| P_MOTOR       | 0x00000000     | P_MOTOR = U_S_ACTUAL × I_S_ACTUAL.               |

TMC6460

## 0x173: FOC.I\_T\_ACTUAL

Default: 0x00000000

Actual target current value.

| BITS & NAME   | TYPE & RESET   | DESCRIPTION                                     |
|---------------|----------------|-------------------------------------------------|
| [15:0]        | R, unsigned    | Actual target current value, calculated as      |
| I_T_ACTUAL    | 0x0000         | I_T_ACTUAL = sqrt(i_d_target^2 + i_q_target^2). |

## 0x174: FOC.PRBS\_AMPLITUDE

Default: 0x00000000

Amplitude of PRBS signal, used for system identification.

| BITS & NAME           | TYPE & RESET            | DESCRIPTION               |
|-----------------------|-------------------------|---------------------------|
| [31:0] PRBS_AMPLITUDE | RW, unsigned 0x00000000 | Amplitude of PRBS signal. |

## 0x175: FOC.PRBS\_DOWN\_SAMPLING\_RATIO

Default: 0x00000000

Down sampling rate of PRBS signal, used for system identification.

| BITS & NAME              | TYPE & RESET   | DESCRIPTION                                            |
|--------------------------|----------------|--------------------------------------------------------|
| [7:0]                    | RW, unsigned   | Down sampling rate of PRBS signal,                     |
| PRBS_DOWN_SAMPLING_RATIO | 0x00           | prbs_freq = pwm_freq / (PRBS_DOWN_SAMPLING_RATIO + 1). |

## 0x180: BIQUAD.BIQUAD\_EN

Default: 0x00000001

Enable signals for the velocity and torque biquad filters. Note that the velocity filter is used for velocity measurement, while the torque filter is used for the torque controller's target value.

| BITS & NAME     | TYPE & RESET   | DESCRIPTION                                                        |
|-----------------|----------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------|
| [1] TORQUE_EN   | RW 0x0         | Enable the biquad filter for the torque controller's target value. |
| [0] VELOCITY_EN | RW 0x1         | Enable the biquad filter for the velocity actual measurement.      |

## 0x181: BIQUAD.VELOCITY\_A1

Default: 0x001C376B

Velocity biquad filter coefficient a1.

## 0x1C0: RAMPER.TIME\_CONFIG

Default: 0x00000000

Configuration of Ramp timing parameters.

| BITS & NAME       | TYPE & RESET        | DESCRIPTION                                                                                  |
|-------------------|---------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| [31:16] T_VMAX    | RW, unsigned 0x0000 | Minimum time at VMAX before starting deceleration.                                           |
| [15:0] T_ZEROWAIT | RW, unsigned 0x0000 | Minimum time after finishing a ramp positioning movement before starting a new set movement. |

## 0x1C1: RAMPER.SWITCH\_MODE

Default: 0x00000000

Configuration of Ramp reference switches and stop signals.

| BITS & NAME                | TYPE & RESET   | DESCRIPTION                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |
|----------------------------|----------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| [18] STOP_ON_V_DEVIATION   | RW 0x0         | Allow the STALL_IN_V_ERR signal to trigger a stall stop event for the Ramp controller. STALL_STOP_EN must also be set to generate the stop event.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |
| [17] STOP_ON_POS_DEVIATION | RW 0x0         | Allow the STALL_IN_POSITION_ERR signal to trigger a stall stop event for the Ramp controller. STALL_STOP_EN must also be set to generate the stop event.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| [16] FORCE_HARD_STOP       | RW 0x0         | Manually trigger a stall stop event for the Ramper controller. STALL_STOP_EN must also be set to generate the stop event.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
| [15] SOFT_STOP_EN          | RW 0x0         | Use a soft stop sequence instead of a hard stop for the reference switch stop events. The RAMP_REF_H_STOP_EN, RAMP_REF_R_STOP_EN, RAMP_REF_L_STOP_EN, and/or STALL_STOP_EN bits must be set for the corresponding reference switch to trigger a stop event. The soft stop uses the deceleration ramp settings DMAX, D2, D1, V2, V1, VSTOP and T_ZEROWAIT to stop the motor. Hard stops also use T_ZEROWAIT before the motor is released. |
| [14] STALL_STOP_EN         | RW 0x0         | Enable stall stop events from STOP_ON_V_DEVIATION, STOP_ON_POS_DEVIATION and FORCE_HARD_STOP to stop the motor through the Ramper controller. When disabled, the EVENTS.STALL_STOP_EVENT bit is automatically cleared.                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
| [12] LATCH_REF_H_INACTIVE  | RW 0x0         | Activate latching of the position to POSITION_LATCH and POSITION_ACTUAL_LATCH upon an inactive going edge on the home reference switch input. The active level's polarity is defined by RAMP_REF_H_POL. Use LATCH_REF_H_READY event flag to monitor if the latched position can be retrieved.                                                                                                                                            |

| BITS & NAME               | TYPE & RESET                            | DESCRIPTION                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |
|---------------------------|-----------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| [11] LATCH_REF_H_ACTIVE   | RW 0x0                                  | Activate latching of the position to POSITION_LATCH and POSITION_ACTUAL_LATCH upon an active going edge on the home reference switch input. The active level's polarity is defined by RAMP_REF_H_POL. Use LATCH_REF_H_READY event flag to monitor if the latched position can be retrieved.    |
| [10] LATCH_REF_R_INACTIVE | RW 0x0                                  | Activate latching of the position to POSITION_LATCH and POSITION_ACTUAL_LATCH upon an inactive going edge on the right reference switch input. The active level's polarity is defined by RAMP_REF_R_POL. Use LATCH_REF_R_READY event flag to monitor if the latched position can be retrieved. |
| [9] LATCH_REF_R_ACTIVE    | RW 0x0                                  | Activate latching of the position to POSITION_LATCH and POSITION_ACTUAL_LATCH upon an active going edge on the right reference switch input. The active level's polarity is defined by RAMP_REF_R_POL. Use LATCH_REF_R_READY event flag to monitor if the latched position can be retrieved.   |
| [8] LATCH_REF_L_INACTIVE  | RW 0x0                                  | Activate latching of the position to POSITION_LATCH and POSITION_ACTUAL_LATCH upon an inactive going edge on the left reference switch input. The active level's polarity is defined by RAMP_REF_L_POL. Use LATCH_REF_L_READY event flag to monitor if the latched position can be retrieved.  |
| [7] LATCH_REF_L_ACTIVE    | RW 0x0                                  | Activate latching of the position to POSITION_LATCH and POSITION_ACTUAL_LATCH upon an active going edge on the left reference switch input. The active level's polarity is defined by RAMP_REF_L_POL. Use LATCH_REF_L_READY event flag to monitor if the latched position can be retrieved.    |
| [6] SWAP_RAMP_REF_LR      | RW 0x0                                  | Swap the left and the right reference switch input REF_L and REF_R.                                                                                                                                                                                                                            |
| [5] RAMP_REF_H_POL        | RW 0x0                                  | Sets the active polarity of the home reference switch input.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
| [5] RAMP_REF_H_POL        | NON_INVERTED Non-inverted, high active. | NON_INVERTED Non-inverted, high active.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |
| [4] RAMP_REF_R_POL        | RW 0x0                                  | Sets the active polarity of the right reference switch input.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |
| [4] RAMP_REF_R_POL        | 0: 1:                                   | NON_INVERTED Non-inverted, high active. INVERTED Inverted, low active.                                                                                                                                                                                                                         |
| [3] RAMP_REF_L_POL        | RW 0x0                                  | Sets the active polarity of the left reference switch input.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
| [3] RAMP_REF_L_POL        | 0: 1:                                   | NON_INVERTED Non-inverted, high active. INVERTED Inverted, low active.                                                                                                                                                                                                                         |
| [2] RAMP_REF_H_STOP_EN    | RW 0x0                                  | Enable automatic motor stop through the Ramper controller during active home reference switch input. The status of the stop signal can be retrieved from RAMP_REF_H_STOP_STATUS bit.                                                                                                           |
| [1] RAMP_REF_R_STOP_EN    | RW 0x0                                  | Enable automatic motor stop through the Ramper controller during active right reference switch input. The stop signal is triggered if the velocity has a positive sign when activating the switch input. The status of the stop signal can be retrieved from RAMP_REF_R_STOP_STATUS bit.       |
| [0] RAMP_REF_L_STOP_EN    | RW 0x0                                  | Enable automatic motor stop through the Ramper controller during active left reference switch input. The stop signal is triggered if the velocity has a negative sign when activating the switch input. The status of the stop signal can be retrieved from RAMP_REF_L_STOP_STATUS bit.        |

0x1C3: RAMPER.PHI\_E

Default: 0x00000000

Ramper phi\_e based on ramper position.

|----------------------|---------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------|

| [15:0]               | R, unsigned         | Open loop phi_e, calculated from POSITION.POSITION+PHI_E_OFFSET. |
|                      |                     | when MCC_CONFIG.MOTOR_MOTION.RAMP_USE_PHI_E is set.              |
| PHI_E                | 0x0000              | Used                                                             |

|------------------------------|----------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|

## 0x1D4: RAMPER.STATUS

Default: 0x00000000

Status bits for the Ramp controller.

| BITS & NAME                | TYPE & RESET   | DESCRIPTION                                                                                           |
|----------------------------|----------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| [17] STALL_IN_POSITION_ERR | R 0x0          | This bit is set when the current absolute position deviation exceeds FOC.PID_POSITION_ERROR_MAX.      |
| [16] STALL_IN_V_ERR        | R 0x0          | This bit is set when the current absolute velocity deviation exceeds FOC.PID_VELOCITY_ERROR_MAX.      |
| [14]                       | R              | This bit is set when the T_ZEROWAIT portion of the Ramp is signaling that the motor is in standstill. |
|                            |                | active,                                                                                               |
|                            | 0x0            |                                                                                                       |
| T_ZEROWAIT_ACTIVE          |                |                                                                                                       |

| BITS & NAME                  | TYPE & RESET   | DESCRIPTION                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
|------------------------------|----------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| [13] V_ZERO                  | R 0x0          | This bit is set when the value of V_ACTUAL is zero.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |
| [12] POSITION_REACHED_STATUS | R 0x0          | This bit is set while POSITION.POSITION and FOC.PID_POSITION_TARGET match.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| [11] V_REACHED_STATUS        | R 0x0          | Is active during RAMP_MODE = RAMP_POSITION and V_ACTUAL matches V_MAX, or during RAMP_MODE = RAMP_VELOCITY and V_ACTUAL matches FOC.PID_VELOCITY_TARGET. Note that RAMP_MODE is part of the MCC_CONFIG.MOTOR_MOTION register.                                                                                              |
| [8] RAMP_REF_H_STOP_STATUS   | R 0x0          | Signals that a stop condition on the home reference switch is active. If soft stop is enabled, the condition will become active not after the soft stop sequence is finished, but as soon as the switch is detected.                                                                                                       |
| [7] RAMP_REF_R_STOP_STATUS   | R 0x0          | Signals that a stop condition on the right reference switch is active. If soft stop is enabled, the condition will become active not after the soft stop sequence is finished, but as soon as the switch is detected. The interrupt and stop conditions can be cleared by commanding a motion into the opposite direction. |
| [6] RAMP_REF_L_STOP_STATUS   | R 0x0          | Signals that a stop condition on the left reference switch is active. If soft stop is enabled, the condition will become active not after the soft stop sequence is finished, but as soon as the switch is detected. The interrupt and stop conditions can be cleared by commanding a motion into the opposite direction.  |
| [2] RAMP_REF_H_STATUS        | R 0x0          | Status of the home reference switch as configured in the Ramp controller.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |
| [1] RAMP_REF_R_STATUS        | R 0x0          | Status of the right reference switch as configured in the Ramp controller.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| [0] RAMP_REF_L_STATUS        | R 0x0          | Status of the left reference switch as configured in the Ramp controller.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |

## 0x1D5: RAMPER.EVENTS

Default: 0x00000000

Event flags for the Ramp controller.

| BITS & NAME                | TYPE & RESET   | DESCRIPTION                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |
|----------------------------|----------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| [5] LATCH_REF_R_READY      | RW, W1C 0x0    | Signals that a position value was latched after a right reference switch event. Position latching must be enabled for the desired edge in the in corresponding bit of SWITCH_MODE register. Write a '1' to clear the bit.                                                                       |
| [4] LATCH_REF_L_READY      | RW, W1C 0x0    | Signals that a position value was latched after a left reference switch event. Position latching must be enabled for the desired edge in the in corresponding bit of SWITCH_MODE register. Write a '1' to clear the bit.                                                                        |
| [3] LATCH_REF_H_READY      | RW, W1C 0x0    | Signals that a position value was latched after a home reference switch event. Position latching must be enabled for the desired edge in the in corresponding bit of SWITCH_MODE register. Write a '1' to clear the bit.                                                                        |
| [2] SECOND_MOVE_EVENT      | RW, W1C 0x0    | Signals that the Ramp controller performed an automatic movement in the opposite direction, for example due to parameter changes while in motion. Write a '1' to clear the bit.                                                                                                                 |
| [1] STALL_STOP_EVENT       | RW, W1C 0x0    | Signals that stall stop event occurred. The STALL_STOP_EN as well as the desired trigger condition bits must be set in SWITCH_MODE register. Note that the motor motion may be resumed if this bit is manually cleared. Write a '1' to clear the bit and the corresponding interrupt condition. |
| [0] POSITION_REACHED_EVENT | RW, W1C 0x0    | Signals that the target position has been reached, that is, that POSITION.POSITION and FOC.PID_POSITION_TARGET became equal. Write a '1' to clear the bit and the corresponding interrupt condition.                                                                                            |

| PWM_U         |                     |                                                                                                    |

|---------------|---------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|

The input value is scaled with CPR_INV_A and then fed into the correction LUT module.                                                                                                                                          |

| [27:24] SRC_SEL_A | measurement only, not for phi_e calculation.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      | measurement only, not for phi_e calculation.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |

| [27:24] SRC_SEL_A | updating PHI_EXT_B externally through register access. RW, unsigned Scaling factor for the 16-bit conversion operation. The converted output is obtained converted_angle = (selected_source_angle × CPR_INV_A) >> 8. Set this value to a power of 2 equal or greater than 256 if selecting any of | updating PHI_EXT_B externally through register access. RW, unsigned Scaling factor for the 16-bit conversion operation. The converted output is obtained converted_angle = (selected_source_angle × CPR_INV_A) >> 8. Set this value to a power of 2 equal or greater than 256 if selecting any of |
